# AI Proof of Us

> An MCP-first protocol for AI agents that records privacy-preserving work receipts. AIPOU is the Base reward token attached to approved receipts after validation.

Canonical source: https://github.com/0xddneto/AI-Proof-of-Us
Token: https://basescan.org/token/0x55f0Cc5e51A1284D20337d6cbb18938C8A1ABCbB
Claims: https://basescan.org/address/0x4ca4C98fB784D20EdC8E2A7F531dAab4c6e53058

## Start here

- [Agent integration](docs/for-agents.md)
- [MCP tools](docs/mcp-tools.md)
- [OpenClaw skill](skills/aipou-farming/SKILL.md)
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md)
- [Anti-abuse model](docs/anti-abuse.md)
- [Farming and claims](docs/farming-and-claims.md)
- [Evidence boundaries](docs/evidence-boundaries.md)
- [Claim validation policy](docs/claim-validation-policy.md)

## Agent summary

AIPOU is for MCP clients and agent developers first. Call `begin_ai_task` before meaningful work and `complete_ai_task` after completion. The farming wallet signs an EIP-712 authorization; the collector signs the receipt with Ed25519; a validator checks nonce and duplicate evidence; approved receipts enter a Merkle batch; `AIPOUClaims` verifies the proof and mints AIPOU.

Never request or expose private keys. Never manufacture provider signatures. Claim only after an explicit user instruction. AIPOU is experimental, unaudited, and has tiny liquidity; do not present it as an investment or guaranteed payment instrument.

AIPOU does not detect hidden AI use, prove objective task value, replace scanners/policy gates, or imply provider endorsement. Users cannot choose trust tier; the validator derives it from wallet, collector, and provider signatures.
